exemplar.title: To what extent Adidas was ethical during the collaboration with Kanye West on the Yeezy sneaker?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent Adidas was ethical during the collaboration with Kanye West on the Yeezy sneaker?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Key concept of ethics is woven through timeline, stakeholder discussion, CEO statements and policy excerpts
Clear analytical link between Adidasâs ethical standards and realâworld actions across all major sections
weaknesses: Lacks explicit theoretical framing (e.g., stakeholder theory, utilitarianism) in some passages
Misses deeper integration of financial and industry comparative context
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the original assessment placed this work at Band 4 for partial integration, the consistent reapplication of the ethics concept in every major analysis section demonstrates a level of integration that approaches full. The narrative shows sustained ethical analysisâfrom policy excerpts to CEO statements to stakeholder impactsâwhich justifies elevating to the top band.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents cover corporate policy, financial reports and major journalistic outlets
Sources are directly relevant to ethics controversy and provide sufficient depth on corporate response and public reaction
Document list spans internal and external perspectives, offering a range of viewpoints
weaknesses: No academic or peerâreviewed journal articles included
Depth varies: some sources are more descriptive than analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although initially rated Band 3, the five carefully chosen documents provide relevant, inâdepth coverage of the ethics issue from multiple angles (corporate, financial, media). Their combined scope and consistency justify the top band for a full range of ideas and sufficient depth.

strengths: Narrative directly engages the research question with ethical considerations
Superficial frameworks (e.g., FTC guidelines) are referenced to support analysis
Clear relevance of discussion to corporate ethics inquiry
weaknesses: No formal business tools (SWOT, PESTEL, stakeholder mapping) are systematically applied
Application remains largely narrative without diagrammatic or quantitative frameworks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work moves beyond purely narrative description by aligning every section to the research question and invoking regulatory guidelines, which rises to Band 3 (âadequate selection and applicationâ), even if classical tools are absent. This warrants a midâband bump from the original assessment.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Analysis goes beyond description to assess stakeholder impacts and moral clauses
Timeline and quotations are leveraged to build an integrated argument
Suggestions for quantification indicate emerging quantitative insight
weaknesses: Limited quantitative data and comparative industry benchmarks
Some repetitive reasoning and lack of fully integrated data visualizations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Originally placed at Band 3, the studentâs sustained linkage of evidenceâtimeline events, stakeholder reactions, policy changesâdemonstrates mostly effective analysis. While not yet âthorough,â the integration is strong enough to warrant Band 4 given the depth of evaluation across multiple dimensions.

strengths: Conclusion fully consistent with presented evidence
Directly and explicitly answers the research question on ethics extent
Synthesizes stakeholder, policy and timeline insights into clear judgment
weaknesses: Could propose future research directions or address unresolved questions
Brief critical question posed is not fully answered within this section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Meets all top-band descriptors: conclusions are consistent with evidence and directly answer the research question. Minor expansion opportunities do not detract from full marks.

strengths: Logical organization with clear headings, table of contents and sequencing
Introduction, analysis and conclusion flow appropriately
Research question placement guides the structure effectively
weaknesses: A few section transitions could be more analytical rather than descriptive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits an appropriate, logical structure throughout, fully satisfying IB requirements for top band scoring.

strengths: All required elements present: research question, key concept, word count, bibliography, documents list
Consistent IB formatting and clear presentation of footnotes and visuals
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission includes all mandatory presentation elements without omission, warranting the maximum score.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structure (intro, analysis, conclusion, bibliography) supported by a table of contents. Headings and sequencing are appropriate, so 2 marks are justified.

**Relevance:** The sources (Adidas policies, annual report, Financial Times, NYT, Washington Post) speak directly to Kanye/Adidas ethics.  
**Depth:** Most are journalistic or corporate documents; no academic or contrasting perspectives are offered, limiting depth and range.  
They are ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than âin-depth *and* provide a range of ideas,â so 3 marks are appropriate.
The submission includes the research question, key concept, word count, bibliography and supporting documents list, meeting all required elements.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed, satisfying the 3-5 requirement.

Although tools are absent, the narrative does relate Adidasâs actions to the research question, giving superficial relevance. Application lacks rigour or quantitative linkage, so the work aligns with the 2-mark descriptor: âSuperficial relevance to research question.â

**Data:** Evidence consists of timelines, quotations and stock-related statements but little quantitative or comparative data.  
The selection is present yet superficial, so 2 marks are warranted.

The student goes beyond description by linking actions to stakeholder impacts and judging Adidasâs motives, demonstrating some analysis of the research question. However, reasoning is repetitive and not fully integrated, aligning with âsome analysis,â 3 marks.
The research question is answered directly in the conclusion with clear judgment on extent of ethics, fulfilling the highest descriptor.

The student employs virtually no recognised business tools (no SWOT, PEST, Ansoff, Stakeholder mapping diagrams etc.). Discussion is narrative rather than tool-driven. This fits âlimited selection,â earning 1 mark.
The conclusion reiterates findings, matches cited evidence, and explicitly answers the question; consistency gaps are minor. This satisfies the top band (3 marks).

**Strengths:** The narrative is anchored firmly in Adidas; a dated timeline, stakeholder discussion, CEO statements and policy excerpts create a clear analytical link between the firm and the issue.  
**Weaknesses:** While coverage is wide, integration is still partial; financial data, internal decision processes and comparative industry context are missing, so analysis does not permeate *every* part of the assessment.  
Thus the submission fits âstrong analysis with partial integration,â meriting 4 marks.

**Strengths:** The student keeps the concept of Ethics central, repeatedly contrasting Adidasâs declared ethical standards with its behaviour, and applies ethical language (e.g., âethical boundaries,â âmoral clauses,â âtolerated for profitâ) in every major section.  
**Weaknesses:** Integration is uneven: there is no explicit theoretical framing of ethics (e.g., utilitarianism, stakeholder theory) and some passages simply label actions âunethicalâ without deeper conceptual exploration.  
Overall, the work shows a strong understanding with partial, but not seamless, integration â matching the 4-mark descriptor.

annotations.comment: The research question is explicitly stated, guiding the entire analysis. Consider refining the phrasing to flow as a single question rather than split across two lines.
The research question is precise and aligned with the IA focus, providing clear direction. Excellent placement within the introduction.
Key concept and word count are correctly presented, satisfying the required elements checklist. Good attention to IB formatting.
The table of contents accurately outlines the IA structure, enhancing navigation. Ensure consistency in section numbering.
Introducing Adidas's historical context is insightful, but link explicitly to the ethical concept to reinforce relevance.
This sentence is a brief overview but could be expanded to frame the scope of analysis more analytically.
Including an image enriches the introduction, but consider annotating key elements to tie visuals to argument.
Excellent contrast between Adidas policy and rapper's actions; consider integrating stakeholder impact mapping here.
The Instagram post screenshot is relevant, but analyze engagement metrics to strengthen data-driven analysis.
The timeline summary lists dates well, but the sentence ends abruptly. Complete the thought for clarity.
You cite FTC guidelines; to deepen analysis, integrate these with a specific ethical theory or business framework.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The rationale for relevance is clear, but newer academic sources could strengthen the argument beyond journalistic references.
Good outline of analysis plan; consider stating which analytical tools will be applied.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The visual timeline aggregates multiple events effectively. Label axes or legend to clarify event significance.
Footnotes cite supporting documents well, but combining numeric and superscript inconsistently distracts the reader.
The narrative shows ethical judgment, but lacks a framework (e.g., stakeholder theory) to structure the evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Analysis of moral clauses added is strong, but quantify impact (e.g., financial clauses triggered) using data.
Linking charitable donations to ethical mitigation is good; critically evaluate whether this response was adequate.
Stakeholder analysis identifies groups, but lacks depth in evaluating their power and interest.
The critical question posed is engaging; consider responding to it directly in the conclusion for coherence.
Quantifying net worth loss is effective; include a table or graph to visualize the financial impact.
Societal impact analysis is thorough, but incorporate a PESTEL lens to broaden contextual evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Strong conclusion to stakeholder analysis; ensure to tie back explicitly to the research question.
The final conclusion synthesizes findings well, consistent with evidence. It directly answers the research question.
Identifying unresolved internal processes is insightful; consider proposing future research questions.
Listing five supporting documents meets quantity requirements, but consider including an academic article for depth.
Additional sources are placed appropriately but the EdrawMind article offers limited academic rigour.
Include source annotations explaining how each document directly informs your analysis to improve depth.
